title: Kirkee War Cemetery
tagline: Kirkee War Cemetery — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: The Kirkee War Cemetery is a cemetery in Khadki (formerly named Kirkee), a cantonment in Pune (formerly Poona) in Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: The Kirkee War Cemetery is a cemetery in Khadki (formerly named Kirkee), a cantonment in Pune (formerly Poona) in Maharashtra, India. The cemetery was created to provide graves for the fallen from central and western India in the Second World War, where there could not be certainty about their graves elsewhere being permanently maintained. The cemetery holds the graves of 1,668 Commonwealth service personnel from World War II, and 629 from World War I who were reburied here from Bombay's Sewri Christian Cemetery in 1962. The graves from Sewri are unmarked and their names are listed on the Kirk

topPlaces.name: Battle of Khadki
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Khadki, also known as or the Battle of Ganeshkhind and Battle of Kirkee, took place at modern day Khadki, India, on 5 November 1817 between the forces of the British East India Company and the Maratha Confederacy under the leadership of Appasaheb Bhonsle. The forces of the East India Company achieved a decisive victory, with Khadki later becoming a military cantonment under British rule.

topPlaces.name: Khadki
topPlaces.description: Khadki is a cantonment in the city of Pune, Maharashtra, India. It has now flourished as a quasi-metropolis & centered in the northern region of the city.

topPlaces.name: CAFVD Sports Stadium
topPlaces.description: The CAFVD Sports Stadium is a stadium in Khadki in Pune. It is located opposite Khadki railway station on old Mumbai-Pune road (Part of NH 4).

topPlaces.name: Erandwane
topPlaces.description: Erandwane is a premium locality in Pune city, and the costliest locality in Pune according to the revised ready reckoner (RR) rates of 2025. This micro-market includes the famous Law College Road, Prabhat Road etc.

topPlaces.name: Kothrud
topPlaces.description: Kothrud (Kothrud Baug during the Maratha Empire era) is an upmarket residential suburb in the city of Pune. Once boasting the tag of Asia's fastest developing suburb, Kothrud of 2025 faces several challenges of urban planning, traffic congestion, environmental degradation, and to some extent, rising crime rates.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Poona
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Pune took place on 25 October 1802 near Pune between the states of the Maratha Confederacy. The forces of the Scindia (Shinde) and the Peshwa Bajirao II were attacked by the Holkars.

topPlaces.name: Shivaji Nagar metro station (Pune)
topPlaces.description: Shivaji Nagar is an underground metro station on the North-South corridor of the Purple Line and North-South corridor of the Pink Line of Pune Metro in Pune, India. The station was opened on 1 August 2023 as an extension of Pune Metro Phase I. Since then, this line was operational between PCMC and Civil Court.

topPlaces.name: Mula River (India)
topPlaces.description: The Mula is a river in Pune, in Maharashtra state of India. It is dammed near the Western Ghats at the Mulshi Dam that forms the Mulshi Lake.

items.note: The Battle of Khadki, also known as or the Battle of Ganeshkhind and Battle of Kirkee, took place at modern day Khadki, India, on 5 November 1817 between the forces of the British East India Company and the Maratha Confederacy under the leadership of Appasaheb Bhonsle. The forces of the East India Company achieved a decisive victory, with Khadki later becoming a military cantonment under British rule.
Khadki is a cantonment in the city of Pune, Maharashtra, India. It has now flourished as a quasi-metropolis & centered in the northern region of the city.
The CAFVD Sports Stadium is a stadium in Khadki in Pune. It is located opposite Khadki railway station on old Mumbai-Pune road (Part of NH 4).

Erandwane is a premium locality in Pune city, and the costliest locality in Pune according to the revised ready reckoner (RR) rates of 2025. This micro-market includes the famous Law College Road, Prabhat Road etc.

Kothrud (Kothrud Baug during the Maratha Empire era) is an upmarket residential suburb in the city of Pune. Once boasting the tag of Asia's fastest developing suburb, Kothrud of 2025 faces several challenges of urban planning, traffic congestion, environmental degradation, and to some extent, rising crime rates.
The Battle of Pune took place on 25 October 1802 near Pune between the states of the Maratha Confederacy. The forces of the Scindia (Shinde) and the Peshwa Bajirao II were attacked by the Holkars.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Shivaji Nagar metro station (Pune) & around

items.note: Shivaji Nagar is an underground metro station on the North-South corridor of the Purple Line and North-South corridor of the Pink Line of Pune Metro in Pune, India. The station was opened on 1 August 2023 as an extension of Pune Metro Phase I. Since then, this line was operational between PCMC and Civil Court.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kirkee War Cemetery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kirkee War Cemetery is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kirkee War Cemetery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pune Airport (7 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (3 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kirkee War Cemetery?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kirkee War Cemetery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kirkee War Cemetery?
faq.a: Stays in Kirkee War Cemetery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kirkee War Cemetery?
faq.a: The top attractions are Battle of Khadki, Khadki, CAFVD Sports Stadium, Erandwane, Kothrud.
